brief: Legs B, and I've set every number tonight off last week's results rather than letting the app copy Jul 15 back at you. Two themes from the week: you keep leaving the last set of leg curls and calf presses unbanked, and your Legs A day is out-performing Legs B on the same machines (15s at 135 on leg extension, 260x15 twice on calves) — so Legs B is the one lagging, not the muscle. RDL: tighter ramp, same 205 top, and I want all four sets at 8 or better; the 15th died at 7 because two of your four sets were spent warming up. Leg curl holds at 135 on purpose — it is the third session there and the fourth set has never been logged; bank all four and it goes to 142.5 next week. Leg extension holds at 142.5 and you chase 15s, which you have already proven at 135. Calves: one ramp at 240 then three honest sets at 280, dead-stop pause at the bottom. Cable crunch is 50.5, which is the real pin — the 50 you logged does not exist on that stack. Plank stays 2 x 60s. One more easy 20-30 min cardio before Sunday.

flags.note: Leg curls and calf presses have both come up a set short two sessions running — banking every set is tonight's actual job.
Legs A is beating Legs B on the shared machines. If tonight feels flat again, say so in a note and I'll rebalance the two days.

overrides.reason: Two of your four sets on the 15th were spent under 190 and the last one died at 7. Tighter ramp, same top — all four at 8 or better.
Your 120 opener was a warm-up in disguise — three real sets at 160, build toward 12s.
Third session at 135 and the fourth set has never made it into the log. Weight holds until all four are banked — then 142.5.
You hit 15s at 135 on Legs A but stall at 12 here. Same weight, chase 15 on every set — that is what unlocks the next step.
You proved 260x15 twice on Saturday — one ramp set, then three real sets at 280. Pause dead at the bottom.
50.5 is the real pin — the 50 you logged does not exist on that stack. You handled it Saturday, so three sets here.
